page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, prioritizing raw processing power over memory capacity.

The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via external network-attached volumes. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from other tenants on the host hardware.

This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities but have modest memory footprints. Suitable workloads include high-performance web serving, batch processing, distributed analytics, and CI/CD pipelines where CPU execution speed is the primary bottleneck.

The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Because the CPU allocation is dedicated, users receive consistent, predictable performance for parallel processing tasks. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities but have modest memory footprints. It is highly suitable for applications such as batch processing, high-performance web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
